teaching_card.title: Linear Word Problems (Hard)
teaching_card.body: Translate the words into an equation. A 'flat fee plus a rate' is total = fee + rate × amount. Subtract the fixed part, then divide by the rate to find the unknown.
key_points: Identify the fixed amount and the per-unit rate.
Undo the fixed part first, then divide.

screens.title: Translate, then solve
screens.body: Turn the words into an equation. 'Flat fee plus a rate' is total = fee + rate·n. 'Sum' means add; 'is/equals' is the = sign; 'more than' and 'times' tell you how the quantities relate.
key_points: Name the unknown, then write an equation.

screens.title: Undo step by step
screens.body: Solve by reversing what was done to the unknown. For total = fee + rate·n, subtract the fee first, then divide by the rate. Do the inverse operations in reverse order.
key_points: Subtract constants first, then divide by the rate.

screens.title: Two related unknowns
screens.body: When two quantities are linked ('one is 12 more than the other,' 'the larger is 3 times the smaller'), write both in terms of one variable, then use the total. e.g., n + 3n = total.
key_points: Express both quantities with one variable.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: A fee of $20 plus $15/hour totals $95: 20 + 15h = 95 -> 15h = 75 -> h = 5. Sum of two numbers is 40 and one is 6 more: larger = (40 + 6)/2 = 23.
